texts.preview_tail: completed text based on the input provided: INTRODUCTION {#s1} ============ Existing evidence suggests that overall survival (OS) can be improved with neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy followed by surgery
texts.full_text: have indicated that overall survival (OS) could be improved using neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy followed by surgery for esophageal cancer \[[@R1]\]. However, neoadjuvant therapy might increase the risk of postoperative morbidity or perioperative mortality \[[@R2]\], so a considerable number of patients with local advanced thoracic esophageal squamous cell carcinoma (SCC) in our country perform surgery as their initial treatment. However, sometimes it is difficult to achieve the purpose of complete resection because of surgeons\' skills. MoreoverHere's the completed text based on the input provided: INTRODUCTION {#s1} ============ Existing evidence suggests that overall survival (OS) can be improved with neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy followed by surgery
